function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'foo' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Is it possible that the loop with condition 'e != 1' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'e != 1' always executed?
Can the loop with condition 'i < 6' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i < 6' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 11:  <statement> if (!x) </statement> is reached, the statement line 12:  <statement> return; </statement> has already been reached?
Is it guaranteed that whenever line 26:  <statement> return 0; </statement> is reached, the statement line 22:  <statement> a = 0; </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable h at line 10: <statement>       int *h[1] = { &g[0] }; </statement> depend on the value of variable g at line 7: <statement>   int g[] = { }; </statement>?
Does variable j at line 19: <statement>   int i, j; </statement> determine the value stored in variable i at line 20: <statement>   for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) </statement>?
liveness: Is variable 'g' live at the end of function 'foo'?
Does variable 'x' remain in use at the end of function 'foo'?
dead_code: Can the program actually reach the statement: “int g[] = { };”?
